rationale: Keep this pair because the citing paper explicitly states it "extends prior algorithm-aversion research centered on consumers' choice between human and algorithmic agents," and the cited paper is precisely a core algorithm-aversion study on task objectivity and interventions (including affective human-likeness). The conceptual overlap is strong (algorithm vs. human decisions, mitigation via human-likeness/anthropomorphism), so this is plausibly foundational rather than an incidental mention; the DOI match confirms the exact cited work.
